authors: Devulder J, De Laat M, Van Bastelaere M, Rolly G
title: Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Valuable Treatment for Chronic Failed Back Surgery Patients
journal_name: Journal of Pain and Symptom Management
study_question: Evaluate long-term outcomes and cost-effectiveness of SCS in chronic failed back surgery syndrome.
population_assessed: 69 chronic failed back surgery syndrome patients treated with spinal cord stimulation.
follow_up_duration: 13-year study period; mean stimulator use 4.9 years
outcome_measures: Pain relief, return to work, drug intake, stimulator use, annual costs
inclusion_criteria: CFBSS, no surgical option, no psychological impairment, successful 2-week trial stimulation
answer_to_study_question: SCS gave sustained pain relief for many; 43 of 69 continued therapy with good relief.
reasoning: All models agreed on bibliographic fields and sample size. Case-series design was favored by two models and better fits an uncontrolled 69-patient follow-up. PubMed link came from one high-confidence model; DOI was unavailable.
field_level_decisions_json: {"study_design": "Chose case_series_uncontrolled over retrospective_cohort_controlled due to two-model agreement and uncontrolled study description.", "follow_up_duration": "Merged consistent details: 13-year study window and mean 4.9-year device use.", "doi": "Returned null because other models indicated unavailable DOI with NAN.", "pubmed_hyperlink": "Accepted OpenRouter value because it was specific and high confidence."}
